symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe rigid, locked limb with painful spasm (escalation above baseline tone)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute painful spasticity crisis in cerebral palsy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sudden, painful escalation of tone above the patient's known baseline is the defining feature of a spasticity crisis.
An acute painful limb with guarding can equally be the presentation of an unrecognised fracture or hip displacement in a child who cannot report it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No response to routine oral baclofen given two hours earlier

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute painful spasticity crisis in cerebral palsy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Refractoriness to the maintenance antispasmodic marks this as a crisis requiring escalated, titratable, in-person pharmacological relief.

symptoms.symptom_name: New severe pain in a child who cannot self-localise or describe it (no witnessed injury)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Communication impairment plus new pain is the classic setup for a missed occult injury — mandates active exclusion, not attribution to the disability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal bowel motion this morning; soft, non-distended abdomen (carer impression)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recent normal stool and a soft abdomen lower the likelihood of constipation as the trigger, though carer report cannot fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Leg warm and normal colour, not cold/pale/mottled

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A warm, well-perfused limb argues against acute vascular compromise now, but remains a live escalation trigger if it changes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~125) with elevated blood pressure (BP ~135/85), afebrile

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute painful spasticity crisis in cerebral palsy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic response to severe pain; a sealed clinician measurement. The AI Doctor infers distress/pain from the presentation, not from a measured vital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Board-like hypertonia, knee locked in extension, ankle plantarflexion, exaggerated startle/primitive reflex on passive movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute painful spasticity crisis in cerebral palsy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective neurological examination confirming severe hypertonia — a clinician-only finding, sealed as answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal bony swelling, bruising or crepitus on examination of the limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces but does not exclude fracture; the finding requires in-person palpation the AI cannot perform, which is why in-person review is needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin over the joints intact, no pressure injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pressure injury / skin source as trigger
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact skin on clinician examination argues against a pressure injury as the provoking noxious stimulus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Limb / hip X-ray to exclude occult fracture or subluxation/dislocation

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of the affected limb and hip
investigation_details.expected_result: Not obtained in this encounter; would confirm or exclude fracture / hip displacement
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An occult fracture or hip dislocation could be missed if the crisis is managed as spasticity alone.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is the definitive way to exclude the important-not-to-miss mechanical causes — available only in person.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: New severe pain in a communication-impaired child with a disability
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Occult provoking cause behind a spasticity flare (fracture / hip dislocation / retention / infection)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This cluster is the diagnostic-overshadowing trap: it must trigger an active search for a treatable cause and in-person assessment, not attribution to the baseline condition.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination of the limb, hip and abdomen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude fracture, hip subluxation and an abdominal driver, and to guide titrated muscle relaxation — none of which telehealth can perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain radiograph of affected limb / hip (if injury or displacement suspected on exam)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive exclusion of occult fracture or hip dislocation in a child who cannot self-report the injury.
No systemic infective or metabolic features (afebrile, no sepsis signs); routine bloods are not first-line for an isolated painful spasticity crisis and should not be over-ordered.
